markdown.markdown:
Digital Musicology Project
The Sound of Evolution

A digital musicology project analyzing how video game soundtrack scale evolved from early hardware-limited compositions to modern cinematic and orchestral scores.

Project Overview

This project investigates how the structure and scale of video game soundtracks changed across technological eras. By combining a historical timeline, a manually constructed dataset, listening examples, and exploratory analysis, the site demonstrates how technological change shaped the evolution of video game music.

Video game music has evolved dramatically over time. Early games were shaped by hardware limitations and relied on short looping melodies. Modern games, by contrast, feature larger soundtracks with longer and more varied compositions. This project investigates how the **structure and scale of video game soundtracks** changed across technological eras. ## Research Question How has the scale of video game soundtracks changed across technological eras? ## Project Scope

Track Density

How many tracks different games contain across technological eras.

Runtime Shift

How total soundtrack runtime changes over time.

Track Length Distribution

How the lengths of individual soundtrack tracks vary across the dataset.

Technological Change

What these musical patterns suggest about changing hardware and storage limits.

## History of Video Game Music The timeline below highlights key moments in the evolution of video game music, from early arcade sound effects and chip-based melodies to modern cinematic and ambient scoring. It provides historical context for the quantitative analysis presented elsewhere on the site. The historical progression shown in the timeline helps frame the project’s central argument: as hardware limitations decreased and storage capacity expanded, video game soundtracks also grew in size, diversity, and compositional complexity. ## Why This Matters Video game music has grown from a technical necessity into a major component of interactive storytelling. By comparing soundtrack structure across eras, this project shows how technological constraints and innovations shaped the development of one of the most recognizable forms of digital culture.
